title: The Most Exciting (and Baffling) Movies of Awards Season, Plus the Genius of ‘Andor’
format: Sean, podcasting through a COVID quarantine, and Amanda dig into Amsterdam, Triangle of Sadness, and Tár, recap the New York Film Festival trip that got Sean sick (White Noise, Master Gardener, Decision to Leave, The Eternal Daughter), check in on Andor, and cover Sean's isolation-era viewing, including Marvel's Werewolf by Night.

segments.heading: Amsterdam
segments.note: David O. Russell's screwball ‘business plot’ comedy

films.title: Amsterdam
films.note: Christian Bale, Margot Robbie, and John David Washington anchor a huge ensemble in a fictionalized take on the real 1933 fascist coup attempt known as the Business Plot. Sean: 'this is a very, very bad movie... it's really rickety and kind of all over the place.' Amanda compares it to a magazine piece that should've been killed with a kill fee before it ran.

segments.heading: Triangle of Sadness
segments.note: Ruben Östlund's Palme d'Or-winning eat-the-rich satire

films.title: Triangle of Sadness
films.note: A supermodel couple (Harris Dickinson, Charlbi Dean) take a luxury yacht vacation that goes very wrong. Both hosts single out Dolly de Leon's third act as a Best Supporting Actress case — 'let's start the Oscar campaign right now' — though Amanda found the first-act Instagram satire too on the nose.

segments.heading: Tár
segments.note: Todd Field's first film in 16 years

films.title: Tár
films.note: Cate Blanchett plays fictional composer-conductor Lydia Tár, whose power and control are put on trial. Amanda calls it 'a fucking cool movie... I feel alive again,' while Sean compares its total command of tone to Field's own apprenticeship on a Kubrick set.

segments.heading: Andor, and the Star Wars it grew out of
segments.note: Tony Gilroy's show, five or six episodes in

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Andor is a direct prequel to this, which was itself a prequel to A New Hope — 'that sounds elaborate and annoying, but it really is not.'

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: the trilogy Rogue One leads into; Sean says Andor barely registers as Star Wars at all — 'no lightsabers here,' no sign of the Skywalkers.

segments.heading: White Noise
segments.note: opening night of the New York Film Festival, at Alice Tully Hall

films.title: White Noise
films.note: Noah Baumbach's Don DeLillo adaptation. Sean says a gas-station sequence is 'literally on the level of Spielberg' and calls it the best filmmaking of Baumbach's career, while noting it's 'definitionally not for everyone.'

segments.heading: More from the New York Film Festival
segments.note: Master Gardener, Decision to Leave, and The Eternal Daughter

films.title: Master Gardener
films.note: Paul Schrader's latest lonely-man redemption story: Joel Edgerton as an ex-neo-Nazi turned horticulturist, Sigourney Weaver as the plantation-owner boss who employs him. Doesn't have distribution yet, but Sean loved it.

films.title: Decision to Leave
films.note: Park Chan-wook's Hitchcock homage, led by Tang Wei. Both hosts liked it without loving it — 'purposefully convoluted,' per Sean, and a bit slow.

films.title: The Eternal Daughter
films.note: Joanna Hogg's mother-daughter ghost story, with Tilda Swinton playing both roles. Sean found it slow and airless; Amanda liked it more but ranks it below Hogg's Souvenir films.

segments.heading: Sean's rainy-Saturday rep screenings
segments.note: a pop-up Pavement museum, then two revival screenings back to back

films.title: King Lear
films.note: Jean-Luc Godard's Cannon Films oddity, with Molly Ringwald, Norman Mailer, Burgess Meredith, and a Woody Allen cameo that got a round of applause from the audience. Sean: 'I wouldn't say I liked it. I'm not sure how one likes a movie like that.'

films.title: Belly
films.note: Hype Williams' Nas/DMX crime film, seen on 35mm at the Metrograph. Sean: 'borderline incoherent' but 'fucking amazing' — DMX and Taral Hicks 'radiant,' Nas 'absolutely atrocious.'

films.title: Werewolf by Night
films.note: Directed by composer Michael Giacchino, starring Gael García Bernal. Sean calls it 'clever' and 'breezy' and wishes Marvel made more of these instead of 'these long drawn out mediocre TV shows.'

segments.heading: What's next
segments.note: Amanda's quarantine watchlist and a coming David Lynch episode

films.title: The New Centurions
films.note: Amanda's isolation watch: a 1970s LA cop drama based on Joseph Wambaugh's novel, part of a deep dive into a book about '70s film.

films.title: Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me
films.note: timed to its 30th anniversary, part of an upcoming David Lynch episode with Adam Naiman.

films.title: Lost Highway
films.note: the other anniversary title (25th) anchoring that same Lynch episode.

referenced.group: The Östlund trilogy Triangle of Sadness completes
Cate Blanchett's last Best Actress win
Todd Field also acted, for Kubrick
South Korea's last international feature winner

Rogue One: A Star Wars Story.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me.title: Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me
